purpose: Prove IAMScope blocks a group-escalation path when identity-policy Deny overrides Allow.
claim_surface: IAMScope can detect a blocked admin-equivalent group escalation path caused by explicit identity-policy Deny.
IAMScope can serialize blocker evidence that attributes the blocked result to identity-policy Deny.
benchmark_proves: For the Env03 alice->admins path, IAMScope emitted one blocked iam_group_membership_escalation finding.
For that path, IAMScope emitted zero validated iam_group_membership_escalation findings.
The blocked finding carried identity_deny blocker evidence and the deny-specific required check failed.
benchmark_does_not_prove: It does not prove all identity-policy Deny cases across all actions and resources.
It does not prove anything about broader admin_reachability or assume_role_chain behavior.
unknowns_remaining: How the same deny logic behaves in more complex multi-hop paths remains unproven here.
False-negative behavior for partially parsed or malformed deny policies remains unknown here.

ground_truth.reason: Explicit identity-policy Deny on iam:AddUserToGroup overrides Allow for env03-alice -> env03-admins.

directly_proven_if_pass: This exact blocked group-escalation path is truthfully detected as blocked.
The blocker attribution includes identity_deny evidence.
strongly_supported_if_pass: The current deny binder + reasoner path is coherent for this narrow real-AWS case.
only_implied_even_if_pass: Broader deny coverage outside this exact path family is only implied, not directly proven.
